design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: Latent-realizability of proximal bridge-null directions. Pre-anchor check: closest anchors are proximal causal inference without unique bridges, the published null-annihilator quotient criterion, and finite negative-control latent-class formulations. Why non-trivial? The target is not the already-solved algebraic row-space criterion and not an LP/Manski bound: require a finite observed negative-control law where the bridge equation has algebraic null directions, but only some null directions are induced by valid latent negative-control causal models with nonnegative latent strata and exclusion restrictions. The theorem should characterize the realizable null cone and prove a non-equivalence witness: two observed laws with the same bridge operator nullspace and same algebraic quotient certificate, but different sharp ATE sets because one null direction is latent-model-realizable and the other is a spurious bridge-fiber artifact. Why promising? The nonroutine object is a realizability certificate separating algebraic bridge fibers from causal latent-model fibers, with a hand-derived 2x3 or 3x3 witness and endpoint-attaining latent laws. If the delta reduces to LP duality, Manski missing-cell bounds, standard null-annihilator algebra, or definition-unfold latent-class feasibility, pivot or accept field-tier.

proposed_from.topic: Latent-realizability of proximal bridge-null directions. Pre-anchor check: closest anchors are proximal causal inference without unique bridges, the published null-annihilator quotient criterion, and finite negative-control latent-class formulations. Why non-trivial? The target is not the already-solved algebraic row-space criterion and not an LP/Manski bound: require a finite observed negative-control law where the bridge equation has algebraic null directions, but only some null directions are induced by valid latent negative-control causal models with nonnegative latent strata and exclusion restrictions. The theorem should characterize the realizable null cone and prove a non-equivalence witness: two observed laws with the same bridge operator nullspace and same algebraic quotient certificate, but different sharp ATE sets because one null direction is latent-model-realizable and the other is a spurious bridge-fiber artifact. Why promising? The nonroutine object is a realizability certificate separating algebraic bridge fibers from causal latent-model fibers, with a hand-derived 2x3 or 3x3 witness and endpoint-attaining latent laws. If the delta reduces to LP duality, Manski missing-cell bounds, standard null-annihilator algebra, or definition-unfold latent-class feasibility, pivot or accept field-tier.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue/banked entry is eid_proximal_bridge_quotient_v1, which was rejected because the bridge-nullspace orthocomplement criterion is already published. The proposal does not revisit that criterion. It holds the quotient certificate fixed and asks whether the latent-realizable null cone R_P changes the sharp ATE set. The downgraded eid_proximal_phase_v1 and failed eid_proximal_completeness_v1 reviews identify exactly this missing finite realization layer: matrix-only null directions and proxy-null endpoint arrays were not enough without full observed-law preservation and latent completions.","published_axis":"The closest published paper is zhang2023proximalNonunique, which resolves algebraic functional invariance under nonunique bridge solutions. The closest finite latent-class paper is shi2020multiplyRobustDNC, which gives categorical negative-control formulas under latent/proxy restrictions. The epsilon-gap is a non-equivalence witness: two observed finite laws have the same algebraic bridge-nullspace and quotient certificate but different sharp ATE sets because R_P differs. ghassami2023partialProxy motivates the partial-ID consumer but does not characterize bridge-null endpoint attainability by latent negative-control laws."}
seed_list: Characterize the latent-realizable proximal bridge-null cone by a finite Farkas certificate and prove it can be strictly smaller than the algebraic bridge kernel.
Give sharp ATE bounds in rank-deficient finite proximal models by optimizing only over latent-realizable bridge-null directions.
Construct two finite observed laws with identical bridge-nullspace quotient certificates but different sharp ATE sets because only one law realizes the null direction latently.
Derive observable determinant-plus-inequality necessary conditions for a proposed bridge-null direction to lie in the image of a finite latent negative-control completion.
Classify minimal 2-by-2, 2-by-3, and 3-by-3 tables into unique-bridge, quotient-identified, latent-realizable partial-ID, and spurious-fiber phases.
Construct endpoint-attaining latent laws and dual infeasibility certificates for algebraic ATE endpoints in finite rank-deficient proximal models.
Identify the minimal latent support size needed to realize each nonzero bridge-null ray in a finite categorical negative-control model.
Prove quotient invariance is insufficient for sharp-set invariance by giving an open class of observed laws with fixed null-annihilator and varying realizable cones.
Give a polynomial-time latent-completion certificate for fixed support sizes that decides whether a bridge-null perturbation is causal-realizable.
Build a finite observed-law diagnostic for applied proximal analyses that rejects bridge-null perturbations unsupported by any negative-control latent law.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize the latent-realizable proximal bridge-null cone by a finite Farkas certificate and prove it can be strictly smaller than the algebraic bridge kernel.

anchor_paper.author: Zhang, Li, Miao, and Tchetgen Tchetgen; Shi, Miao, Nelson, and Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Zhang et al. handle nonunique bridge algebra, while Shi et al. impose finite categorical latent negative-control structure.
anchor_paper.location: Zhang et al. abstract; Shi et al. Section 3
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Solutions to the integral equations may not be unique, which complicates estimation and inference.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/sharpness-failure is stronger because the target is Zhang et al.'s named quotient criterion and Shi et al.'s finite latent model.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from finite LP duality because latent completions are just another feasible polytope.
seed_details.rebuttal: The gap is not feasibility alone; the certificate must project signed bridge-null tangents while preserving the full observed Y law, the natural attempt flagged as failing in the gaps payload.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 because their null-annihilator criterion is algebraic and does not characterize which kernel rays are induced by nonnegative latent negative-control laws.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: Farkas machinery is used to define a new projected cone and a separation certificate, not to restate generic LP bounds.

adversarial_referee.instance: The cone could be named but not exhibited on a finite observed law.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Section 9 includes a 2-by-3 exhibit computing T, v, and the algebraic segment from primitives.

adversarial_referee.instance: A sufficient latent-completion test would not be flagship.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The conjecture is iff via Farkas separation, including the nonliftable converse.

adversarial_referee.instance: Zhang et al. may already allow nonunique bridges without uniqueness.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The draft explicitly preserves their algebraic result and targets latent liftability absent from that comparator.

seed_details.burned_reason: Angle 0 failed to repair the same-quotient/different-sharp-set witness and Farkas certificate; angle 1 rejected field-tier. Reviewers found invalid finite witness algebra, generic Farkas projection, and no flagship characterization beyond latent-class feasibility.

seed_details.one_line: Give sharp ATE bounds in rank-deficient finite proximal models by optimizing only over latent-realizable bridge-null directions.

anchor_paper.author: Ghassami, Shpitser, and Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Completeness failure motivates bounds without identifying a bridge function.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: we propose partial identification methods that do not require completeness

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Default-to-LP risk is controlled only if endpoints include latent-completion witnesses and dual nonrealizability certificates.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Ghassami et al.'s proxy bounds because they already handle non-completeness.
seed_details.rebuttal: Ghassami et al. bypass bridge functions; this seed targets the middle regime where bridge fibers exist but endpoint directions require latent realization.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2024 because their bounds obviate bridge identification rather than characterizing latent-realizable bridge-null endpoints.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if endpoints are certified by latent-completion witnesses; otherwise lands as generic LP and should downgrade.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could read as another finite LP bound.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Require endpoint-attaining latent laws and dual obstructions for algebraic endpoints.

adversarial_referee.instance: The same object may bound ATT or policy contrasts.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: ATE is the flagship focal object; extensions can be listed as corollaries if the same cone works.

adversarial_referee.instance: Partial-proxy bounds may already include this finite model.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The gaps payload says they use different nonsmooth bounds and do not prove finite realization of bridge endpoints.

seed_details.burned_reason: Angle 0 failed to repair the same-quotient/different-sharp-set witness and Farkas certificate; angle 1 rejected field-tier. Reviewers found invalid finite witness algebra, generic Farkas projection, and no flagship characterization beyond latent-class feasibility.

seed_details.one_line: Construct two finite observed laws with identical bridge-nullspace quotient certificates but different sharp ATE sets because only one law realizes the null direction latently.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith banked rejection and Zhang, Li, Miao, Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: The quotient criterion is already solved algebraically, leaving latent-model-induced null directions open.
anchor_paper.location: README banking reason / gaps rejection suggested_directions[1]
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: latent negative-control causal models rather than merely by algebraic bridge fibers

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Witness-on-single-law risk is handled by pairing two laws with identical algebraic certificates and different sharp sets.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just the banked quotient result in disguise.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed fixes the quotient certificate across both laws and varies only latent-realizable directions, exactly sidestepping the burned quotient criterion.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not eid_proximal_bridge_quotient_v1 because quotient equivalence is held fixed while sharp ATE sets differ.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the contribution is a non-equivalence witness between algebraic bridge fibers and causal latent fibers.

adversarial_referee.instance: The witness could rely on zero cells or collapsed outcomes.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The draft demands positive finite cells and endpoint-attaining latent laws.

adversarial_referee.instance: Comparator might target functional identification, not sharp sets.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: That distinction is the point: the statement separates functional quotient ID from sharp-set equivalence.

adversarial_referee.instance: A single pair may be field-tier.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Promote to an open class if the 2-by-3 pair alone is judged too narrow.

seed_details.one_line: Derive observable determinant-plus-inequality necessary conditions for a proposed bridge-null direction to lie in the image of a finite latent negative-control completion.

anchor_paper.author: Miao, Geng, and Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Proxy identification can rely on rank-style restrictions without identifying the full latent measurement model.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: rank conditions even when the full latent measurement model is not identified

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens assumption X is weaker unless the inequalities separate rank/completeness from latent realizability; this seed names that separation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a routine rank-completeness diagnostic.
seed_details.rebuttal: The gaps payload says row-rank/support positivity fail because the requested object is the image of latent completions paired with an explicit bridge.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not MiaoGengTchetgen2018 because it targets null-direction realizability rather than point identification from proxy rank conditions.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS only if the determinant/minor system proves a gap from standard rank checks.

adversarial_referee.instance: Necessary conditions alone may not characterize realizability.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Lands partly; keep as secondary unless upgraded to iff.

adversarial_referee.instance: No sharp set or endpoint map.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use as diagnostic lemma supporting Conjecture 1, not the flagship headline.

adversarial_referee.instance: Latent class literature may contain algebraic tests.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Deepening did not surface a proximal bridge-null realizability test in the supplied map.

seed_details.one_line: Classify minimal 2-by-2, 2-by-3, and 3-by-3 tables into unique-bridge, quotient-identified, latent-realizable partial-ID, and spurious-fiber phases.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal ledger
anchor_paper.claim: Prior reviewers requested valid finite examples exhibiting all proximal phases.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: finite examples exhibiting all three phases

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample-search is stronger only if it exhausts minimal cases; isolated examples were already rejected.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a catalogue of toy examples, not a theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: A complete minimal-support classification with exhaustion proof would answer the reviewer demand for valid observed laws.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not prior matrix-only witnesses because it requires valid observed laws, latent completions, and obstructions for every phase.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if the minimal taxonomy proves exhaustion; otherwise downgrade to field-tier examples.

adversarial_referee.instance: Small tables may be hand-tuned corners.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Require positive cells and minimality/exhaustion, not one table.

adversarial_referee.instance: Classification lacks a general focal object.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The focal object is the phase partition, but this is weaker than the cone certificate.

adversarial_referee.instance: Claims generic-class sharpness but gives only minimal cases.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Lands; use as support for the main witness, not chosen headline.

seed_details.one_line: Construct endpoint-attaining latent laws and dual infeasibility certificates for algebraic ATE endpoints in finite rank-deficient proximal models.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith reviewer ledger and Ghassami, Shpitser, Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Endpoint sharpness in the bridge-fiber middle regime requires finite realization proof.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: finite realization of every proxy-null array was missing

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Default-to-LP framing is a risk; novelty comes only from endpoint-attaining latent laws and dual obstruction.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is standard Balke-Pearl finite response-type sharpness.
seed_details.rebuttal: The object is not a generic response-type LP but the bridge-null endpoint map constrained by negative-control latent exclusions.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not generic LP sharpness because algebraic bridge endpoints are filtered by latent negative-control realizability.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS only with explicit endpoint witnesses and nonrealizable endpoint duals.

adversarial_referee.instance: May lack a non-LP representation.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use as a theorem supporting Conjecture 1 rather than the main object.

adversarial_referee.instance: Endpoint witnesses prove sharpness only for listed endpoints.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Needs dual converse for every excluded algebraic endpoint.

adversarial_referee.instance: Ghassami bounds may already be sharp.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Their target avoids bridge identification; this seed conditions on a bridge-fiber parameterization.

seed_details.one_line: Identify the minimal latent support size needed to realize each nonzero bridge-null ray in a finite categorical negative-control model.

anchor_paper.author: Shi, Miao, Nelson, and Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Finite categorical negative-control adjustment provides the relevant latent-class substrate.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: categorical double-negative-control identification uses finite latent/confounder and negative-control structure

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends finite support bookkeeping; stronger only if a sharp support threshold changes realizability.
seed_details.referee_objection: Support-size bounds are Caratheodory-style routine convex geometry.
seed_details.rebuttal: The support threshold is secondary unless it creates a sharp phase boundary for realizability.

seed_details.one_line: Prove quotient invariance is insufficient for sharp-set invariance by giving an open class of observed laws with fixed null-annihilator and varying realizable cones.

anchor_paper.author: Zhang, Li, Miao, and Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: The quotient/null-annihilator criterion is algebraic and concerns functional identification.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Solutions to the integral equations may not be unique

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Witness-trivial risk is addressed by open-class promotion rather than one hand-tuned pair.
seed_details.referee_objection: Open-class promotion may be technically expensive and under-specified.
seed_details.rebuttal: The 2-by-3 witness can be thickened by strict positivity and strict dual slack if the Farkas obstruction is stable.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 because it preserves the null-annihilator while changing the latent-realizable cone.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if the open class is characterized by strict inequalities, not an assumed generic set.

adversarial_referee.instance: Open class might collapse to a single boundary example.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Require strict positivity and strict Farkas slack.

adversarial_referee.instance: No computable evaluation map if open set is vague.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use the Farkas certificate from the chosen seed as the map.

adversarial_referee.instance: May only prove one direction of invariance failure.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: State as non-equivalence, not iff, unless the converse is proved.

seed_details.one_line: Give a polynomial-time latent-completion certificate for fixed support sizes that decides whether a bridge-null perturbation is causal-realizable.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith reviewer ledger
anchor_paper.claim: Prior reviewers requested the image of latent completions paired with an explicit bridge.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: image of latent completions paired with an explicit h

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Algorithmic-identifiability is stronger if complexity and certificate are explicit; weaker if just finite feasibility.
seed_details.referee_objection: Fixed-support feasibility is just linear programming.
seed_details.rebuttal: The decision problem projects latent completions onto bridge-null perturbations and returns both primal lift and dual obstruction.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not the prior completeness proposal because it decides null-direction realizability, not completeness of the bridge operator.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if the output certificate is the new object; field-tier if it is merely 'solve LP'.

adversarial_referee.instance: Algorithm may be a wrapper around LP.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Name the projected certificate and include complexity plus primal/dual outputs.

adversarial_referee.instance: Same certificate may decide ATE, ATT, and bridge existence.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Scope to null-direction realizability; family extension is leverage, not headline.

adversarial_referee.instance: Finite latent class algorithms may already exist.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: No supplied anchor decides proximal bridge-null perturbation liftability.

seed_details.one_line: Build a finite observed-law diagnostic for applied proximal analyses that rejects bridge-null perturbations unsupported by any negative-control latent law.

anchor_paper.author: Zivich, Cole, Edwards, Mulholland, Shook-Sa, and Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Applied proximal analyses require hard-to-verify negative-control validity and completeness assumptions.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: assumptions about negative-control validity and completeness tied to a plausible unmeasured confounder

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Applied diagnostic is weaker unless tied to a theorem-level realizability certificate.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an applied checklist rather than an econometric theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: Use only as consumer-facing corollary of the certificate, not as chosen flagship kernel.

proposed_from.literature_map: Stage -1.1 supplied the map and subsumed the legacy broad scan: Miao-Geng-Tchetgen Tchetgen (2018, Biometrika, miao2018proxyVariables) is the finite proxy identification anchor; Miao-Shi-Li-Tchetgen Tchetgen (2024, Statistical Theory and Related Fields, miao2018confoundingBridge) formalizes outcome bridge functions; Shi-Miao-Nelson-Tchetgen Tchetgen (2020, JRSSB, shi2020multiplyRobustDNC, doi:10.1111/rssb.12361) gives categorical double-negative-control formulas and the closest finite latent-class substrate; Cui-Pu-Shi-Miao-Tchetgen Tchetgen (2024, JASA, cui2024semiparametricProximal, doi:10.1080/01621459.2023.2191817) develops semiparametric proximal ATE theory; Zhang-Li-Miao-Tchetgen Tchetgen (2023, Statistics & Probability Letters, zhang2023proximalNonunique, doi:10.1016/j.spl.2023.109836) is the already-solved algebraic nonunique-bridge/functional-identification anchor; Ghassami-Shpitser-Tchetgen Tchetgen (2024, arXiv, ghassami2023partialProxy, doi:10.48550/arXiv.2304.04374) supplies partial-ID proxy bounds without completeness; Kallus-Mao-Uehara (2022, arXiv, kallus2021minimaxNegativeControls) and weak-functional work address estimator behavior, not causal latent-fiber realizability; Zivich et al. (2023, AJE, zivich2023introducingPCI, doi:10.1093/aje/kwad077) motivates why negative-control assumptions must map to credible latent confounding; the live gap is the finite causal-realizability layer between observed bridge fibers and latent negative-control laws.

banked_reason: Stopped after the final budgeted attempt failed to reach flagship: angle 0 stayed field-tier and needed a pivot because its finite witness/Farkas certificate was invalid, then angle 1 rejected at field tier; the latent-realizability topic remained a generic finite LP/projection feasibility problem without a verified nonroutine witness.

seeds_burned.one_liner: Characterize the latent-realizable proximal bridge-null cone by a finite Farkas certificate and prove it can be strictly smaller than the algebraic bridge kernel.
seeds_burned.reason: Angle 0 failed to repair the same-quotient/different-sharp-set witness and Farkas certificate; angle 1 rejected field-tier. Reviewers found invalid finite witness algebra, generic Farkas projection, and no flagship characterization beyond latent-class feasibility.

seeds_burned.one_liner: Give sharp ATE bounds in rank-deficient finite proximal models by optimizing only over latent-realizable bridge-null directions.
seeds_burned.reason: Angle 0 failed to repair the same-quotient/different-sharp-set witness and Farkas certificate; angle 1 rejected field-tier. Reviewers found invalid finite witness algebra, generic Farkas projection, and no flagship characterization beyond latent-class feasibility.
